source.summary: Candidate portal resume upload contained active API key and short-lived service token
source.description:
Issue summary: A resume upload submitted through a third-party candidate portal contained a plaintext Redwood API key and an ephemeral service token in the resume text. Initial detection occurred via automated DLP patterns that flag high-entropy strings in uploads. Impact: potential exposure of a scoped API key and short-lived token associated with a non-privileged service account used by a CI job. Immediate concern is unauthorized access to internal test harness endpoints and the possibility of lateral token exchange.

Initial detection: 2026-03-09 08:14 UTC by DLP pipeline (upload-scan). The file was quarantined by the vendor but a copy was forwarded to our S3 for analysis (retention policy triggered).

Environment: Candidate portal operated by vendor TalentFlow (third-party SaaS). Upload landed in vendor-managed bucket and vendor forwarded a quarantine copy into our intake bucket in prod/us-east for manual review. The exposed API key appears to be a scoped key used by an internal integration (label: ci-integration-readonly) and the token appears to be a 1-hour service token created via internal token-exchange flow for onboarding automation.

High-level risk: Medium-High until key/token are rotated and audit completed. Potential for ephemeral access to internal test endpoints and leakage to unknown external actors if the file was downloaded before quarantine.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Candidate submits resume via TalentFlow portal (file types: .pdf, .docx, .txt)
2) Vendor's upload-scan DLP detects high-entropy string and quarantines file; vendor notifies us and forwards quarantine copy to Redwood intake S3 bucket
3) DLP pipeline flags plaintext-looking API token pattern and creates automated alert
4) Manual analysis confirms token pattern and matching prefix to internal 'ci-integration-readonly' key
logs: 2026-03-09T08:14:12Z upload-scan INFO file_quarantined key=uploads/2026/03/09/resume_7842.pdf vendor=talentflow quarantine_id=Q-9a7b
2026-03-09T08:15:03Z dlp-service WARN pattern_match high_entropy_string match=AKIA...-CI-INTEG (truncated) severity=high
investigation_notes: Confirmed extracted string matches format of internal API keys issued for CI integrations (prefix AKIA- style used by legacy tooling).

Token exchange logs show a service token (short-lived) was minted on 2026-03-09T06:48:22Z for service-account onboarding-bot with TTL ~1h. Token was used to call /internal/test-harness from IP 203.0.113.45 between 06:49-06:52 UTC.

Vendor logs indicate file was uploaded from IP 198.51.100.23 and quarantined within 2 minutes; unclear whether file was previously uploaded elsewhere before quarantine. Requested full access logs from TalentFlow (pending).
No successful anomalous writes detected in production; calls observed were limited to read-only test harness endpoints. However further auditing required for linked tokens and rotation history.
comments: Ava Chen (Security): Initial alert from DLP. Quarantined copy available in s3://redwood-intake/quarantine/Q-9a7b. Assigning to SecOps for immediate key rotation guidance.
Liam O'Connor (SecOps): Taking this. Initial action: identify key owner, disable / rotate key, revoke associated tokens. Adding SRE for potential traffic block. — 2026-03-09
Priya Nair (SRE): Blocked IP 203.0.113.45 at edge and placed rate-limits on internal test-harness. Confirmed no errors in prod logs beyond read-only test calls. — 2026-03-09 12:10
Ethan Morales (IAM): Located API key id: ci-integration-readonly-2734. Marked for rotation and immediate disable. Created PR to rotate key and update CI jobs. — 2026-03-09 13:02
Maya Patel (Legal/Compliance): Please capture retention request and vendor SLA terms. We may need to notify affected parties if evidence of exfiltration surfaces. — 2026-03-09 14:20
TalentFlow support (vendor): Confirmed quarantine occurred; provided temporary signed URL for quarantine copy. Sending full upload and access logs per request. — 2026-03-10
source.resolution:
Mitigation steps taken: 1) Disabled and rotated exposed API key (ci-integration-readonly-2734) and rolled new key to CI owners (PR merged: ci-configs/pull/4212). 2) Revoked short-lived onboarding token and invalidated all active tokens issued to onboarding-bot. 3) Blocked suspect IPs and requested full access logs from TalentFlow; vendor provided logs and confirmed single upload. 4) SRE verified no anomalous writes to prod and enforced stricter egress rules for test-harness. 5) Created detection signature in DLP to catch similar token patterns and added rule to quarantine any candidate uploads with credential-like patterns. 6) Notified Legal and HR to evaluate whether candidate notification is required (candidate contact info redacted for privacy).

Follow-ups: rotate legacy token prefixes across all CI integrations (task assigned), update onboarding automation to avoid producing long-lived tokens, add vendor SSO checklist to third-party onboarding. Incident classified as closed after follow-up items completed.

source.summary: Ephemeral CI runner initContainer crashes due to Vault token refresh race during secret rewrap
source.description:
Issue summary: Several ephemeral CI runners failed to finish job startup during the secrets rewrap event performed by Secrets Team (2026-03-10 01:13 UTC). The runner initContainer that fetches repo credentials from Vault exits with a 401/permission denied error and the pod moves to CrashLoopBackOff.
Impact: A subset (~8%) of ephemeral runners in prod-us-west failed to complete job bootstrapping for ~45 minutes, blocking CI jobs across multiple repos for internal teams. No user-facing product outages, but CI capacity degraded and release pipelines were delayed.
Environment: prod cluster (us-west), runner autoscaling group: ephemeral-runner-*-pool, Vault path: secret/ci/repos/*, Vault cluster: vault-prod-02.
Observed pattern: Failures correlated with a scheduled Vault rewrap (transit rekey) that rotated wrapping keys and reissued some service tokens. Affected pods show initContainer failing immediately when attempting to exchange the mounted token for Vault session credentials. A manual pod restart usually recovers the runner.
source.steps_to_reproduce:
1) Trigger a Vault rewrap (or simulate token re-issue) while an ephemeral runner pod is in the init stage (initContainer running).
2) initContainer (vault-agent/credentials-fetcher) requests /v1/auth/token/renew or /v1/secret/data/ci/repos/foo with mounted token.
3) Vault returns 401 or error: "invalid token" for the mounted token for a short window.
4) initContainer exits non-zero; Kubernetes restarts the pod and it sometimes recovers after a fresh token is provisioned.
source.logs_and_snippets:
kubectl logs pod/ephemeral-runner-ht2k -c init-fetcher --previous:
2026-03-10T01:14:09Z client=init-fetcher level=info msg=attempting to read secret path=secret/ci/repos/repo-abc
2026-03-10T01:14:09Z client=init-fetcher level=error msg=vault read error status=401 body='{"errors":["invalid token"]}'
2026-03-10T01:14:09Z client=init-fetcher level=error msg=exiting code=2
---
vault-audit (excerpt):
time=2026-03-10T01:14:09 path=auth/token/lookup token_accessor=xxxx result=permission_denied
source.investigation_notes:
Initial hypothesis: race between Vault wrapping/rekey operation and the ephemeral projected service account token refresh path. The mounted token TTL and the vault-agent's token cache are misaligned: during rewrap a token accessor is briefly invalidated while Vault finishes key rotation, causing immediate 401 responses for clients using the old accessor. Secondary hypothesis: the initContainer code path does not implement retry on auth errors and treats 401 as fatal. Observed that kubelet projected tokens were rotated at the same minute (kubelet logs show service account token refresh). Correlation with Secrets Team's rewrap job (job id: secrets-rewrap-20260310) confirmed.
Partial findings: - No evidence of Vault ACL changes; ACL stable. - Restarting the pod often results in success (fresh token mount). - Some nodes saw more frequent failures suggesting timing window depends on node clock and kubelet token refresh timing.
comments: 2026-03-10 02:10 - Priya Desai (SRE): Confirmed >50 runner restarts in 30m window on prod-us-west. Pushed alert to #sre and #ci-ops.

2026-03-10 03:05 - Miguel Alvarez (Secrets Team): Vault rewrap completed at 2026-03-10T01:13:30Z. We saw a short period where token accessors were being reissued; investigating whether our rewrap clients invalidated accessors too eagerly.

2026-03-11 09:20 - Marco Ruiz (Assignee): Reproduced locally by running a small script that revokes token accessor while initContainer is running; confirmed initContainer exits on 401 without retry. Opened short-lived PR to add exponential backoff retry in init container (see related_github_prs).

2026-03-12 11:40 - Ava Chen (Reporter): Prod mitigation applied: added a 2m grace window to the rewrap orchestration and scheduled a rolling restart of ephemeral-runner nodes in the affected pool; failure rate dropped to <0.2% in next run.
source.workaround:
1) Temporarily delay Vault rewraps for the ephemeral-runner node pool by 2 minutes of the planned window to allow in-flight pods to finish init.
2) For currently failing pods: kubectl rollout restart daemonset/ephemeral-runner and/or delete the CrashLoopBackOff pod to force fresh token mount.
3) For urgent CI capacity: scale up dedicated-runner pool to compensate while we stabilize.
source.resolution: In progress. Temporary mitigation (grace window + rolling restarts) reduced failures to near-zero. A code change to the initContainer to implement idempotent retries on Vault auth failures and to treat 401 as retryable during rotation window has been opened (PR referenced above).
source.next_steps: 1) Merge and rollout initContainer retry logic (PR 124) and bump image tag for ephemeral-runner. 2) Add automated 'rotation-safe' pause hook to the Secrets Team orchestration so rewraps can detect active initContainers via a small API on the runner agent. 3) Update runbook with steps for pausing rewraps and for forced restart of runner pods. 4) Postmortem to be drafted if residual impact >5% of runner capacity; deadline 2026-03-20.

source.summary: Vendor payment on hold: invoice currency / bank instruction mismatch for EU consulting vendor
source.description: Issue summary: AP placed invoice NL-2026-1587 on hold. Invoice currency is EUR but vendor provided USD bank instructions. Finance Ops requires correct EUR IBAN/SEPA details and confirmation whether vendor expects NET30 from invoice date or payment on receipt. Impact: vendor is requesting payment escalation due to quarter close; potential relationship escalation if not resolved by SLA. Environment: production payments for EU vendors; payment run scheduled 2026-03-12.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Receive invoice via vendor portal (invoice attached).
2) Match invoice to PO: no PO was created for this consulting engagement (one-off SOW).
3) AP attempted to initiate wire using bank details on PDF (USD account).
4) Treasury blocked payment because currency mismatch flagged by our payment rails and tax team (withholding risk).
5) AP placed payment on hold and escalated to Finance Helpdesk.
source.impact: Payment delayed for €12,450. Vendor has sent follow-up emails and threatened to delay deliverables. Reputational risk with strategic EU consulting partner. Possible late fee or termination of ongoing SOW if unpaid by 2026-03-15.
comments: 2026-03-09 09:12 — Ethan Park: Raised after treasury flagged currency mismatch. Vendor email thread attached. Requesting AP/Treasury to confirm required bank details and tax withholding rules for Sweden-registered vendor.

2026-03-09 10:05 — Treasury (Noah Kim): We cannot route EUR into the USD account on file. Need IBAN/SEPA or vendor must accept USD send with beneficiary conversion. Also flagged tax registration: vendor missing VAT ID in Coupa vendor record.
2026-03-10 08:45 — Vendor (K. Johansson): Vendor confirms they accept EUR via SEPA and provides new IBAN (attached). Requests expedited payment; says USD account is for US invoices only.

2026-03-10 11:30 — AP (Maya Patel): Updated vendor record with provided IBAN, but PO/Cost center mapping missing for this engagement — accounting cannot route to a department code. Asking Engagement Owner to approve cost center mapping.

2026-03-11 09:22 — Finance Ops (Sofia Ruiz): Engagement owner (R&D platform team) approved cost center mapping via Slack thread. AP to re-run invoice match. Treasury to confirm SEPA wire capability for next payment run.
investigation_notes: AP initial check: invoice amount and VAT calculation appear consistent with SOW; invoice not taxed at source per vendor note.
Vendor record had missing VAT ID and only a USD bank account in Coupa. Vendor supplied a new IBAN in email (verified domain matches vendor).
Risk: If we send USD to vendor's USD account for an EUR invoice, vendor may charge conversion and dispute our accounting. Treasury prefers to pay in invoice currency when possible.
Related issue INT-7220 captured a similar case where SEPA payments failed due to beneficiary name formatting; recommend validating IBAN with payment provider before routing.
Payment run window closes 2026-03-12 17:00 UTC; need confirmation from Treasury by 2026-03-11 16:00 to include in run.
action_items: 1) AP: Validate IBAN with treasury and update payment instruction in vendor master (due 2026-03-11 12:00).
2) Treasury: Confirm SEPA routing and any required beneficiary name normalization (due 2026-03-11 16:00).
3) AP/Accounting: Create a one-off internal PO or assign cost center for SOW delivery to enable payment (due 2026-03-11).
4) Finance Ops: Inform vendor of expected payment date and confirm whether late fees apply (due 2026-03-11 end of day).
resolution_notes: 2026-03-11 15:50 — Treasury confirmed SEPA routing OK after IBAN validation. Beneficiary name normalized per payment provider guidance.
2026-03-11 16:10 — AP created internal PO PO-INT-4301 and mapped cost center CC-RND-07 as approved by engagement owner.
2026-03-11 16:35 — Payment re-submitted for 2026-03-12 SEPA run. Vendor notified: expected credit 2026-03-13 CET. No late fees applied per vendor email.
Follow-up: Add vendor VAT ID to master record and update vendor onboarding checklist to require currency-specific bank instructions for EU vendors. Created a task INT-7380 for process change.
source.final_status: Resolved in payment run — vendor paid via SEPA on 2026-03-13

source.summary: Ambiguous legal-entity on consolidated vendor invoice causing tax allocation hold
source.description:
Issue summary: A consolidated invoice (Vendor: GlobalCloud Services) was uploaded to the AP pipeline on 2026-03-02. The PDF contains charge lines attributable to two Redwood legal entities (Redwood Inference, Inc. - US and Redwood Inference Ltd. - UK) but the supplier did not provide entity-level purchase order (PO) mapping or VAT breakdown. The invoice was flagged by tax-validation as 'ambiguous legal entity' and moved to a manual hold queue.

Impact: Payment for the invoice (approx. $142,300 USD / £84,600 GBP equivalent) is blocked for both legal entities. Vendor has emailed requesting confirmation of payment date. This affects month-end close for the UK entity and may induce late-payment fees. Finance needs a deterministic allocation before releases to avoid misposting to tax and GL accounts.

Background: Vendor regularly issues consolidated monthlies that include UK professional services (GBP) and US cloud credits (USD). Historically the vendor provided a split invoice, but this month they consolidated without indicating which line items belong to each registered entity. AP automation dropped the invoice into manual review due to missing VAT/GL attributes and mismatched supplier legal names on the header vs. line items.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Upload consolidated vendor PDF to AP intake (tested with staging AP connector)
2) AP OCR extracts header 'GlobalCloud Services' and line items but no explicit legal-entity tag
3) Tax validation rule 'entity-mapping-required-for-multi-currency' triggers and sets invoice.status = HOLD
4) Invoice routed to manual queue 'AP - Entity Allocation' and triggers notification to finance@redwood.com
source.logs:
AP pipeline log excerpt (2026-03-02T08:42:12Z): ERROR tax-validator:ENTITY_MAP_MISSING invoice_id=GC-2026-03-01-339 action=HOLD reason='multiple currencies, no entity allocation' user=ap-processor-2
AP OCR confidence: 0.78 for supplier name, VAT field empty. Payment scheduler prevented scheduling run 'weekly-payrun-03-05' for related vendor account. Email from vendor received 2026-03-03 14:10 (support@globalcloud.example) stating 'we consolidated per your request' but no split attached.
source.investigation_notes:
- Initial triage by AP (Mar 4): invoice contains 23 lines; 9 lines clearly GBP services, 14 lines appear to be USD cloud credits but no PO numbers for four GBP lines.
- Hypothesis 1: OCR omitted a small 'billing entity' footer on page 2; request a higher-quality original PDF from vendor (sent 2026-03-04).
- Hypothesis 2: Vendor intentionally consolidated invoices for efficiency but expects us to allocate by product code mapping; product codes don't map cleanly to legal entities in our GL.
- AP ran a heuristics allocation (based on currency and product code) that suggested 87% USD / 13% GBP allocation, but tax team rejected as insufficient evidence for VAT reporting.
- Tax requested supplier-signed allocation or invoice with entity-specific VAT numbers per UK HMRC guidance.
- Related past ticket INT-151234 indicates vendor has previously misunderstood our PO instructions; contact history attached.
comments: 2026-03-04 09:12 - Ava Chen (AP): Uploaded original PDF to ticket and flagged for tax review. Requested vendor to provide entity split and VAT numbers. (attachment: GC-2026-03-original.pdf)

2026-03-04 13:45 - Priya Rao (Tax): Confirmed hold. Cannot accept heuristic allocation for VAT reporting. Need supplier-signed split or individual invoices per legal entity. Escalated to Vendor Ops to request corrected docs.

2026-03-05 10:02 - Vendor Ops (Liam O'Connor): Vendor responded; attached 'statement' file but still no legal-entity mapping. Vendor claims contract references 'Redwood' without legal entity differentiation. Asked procurement to confirm PO routing rules. (see linked_conversation #vendor-ops)

2026-03-08 16:22 - Samuel Patel (AP Lead): Ran manual allocation worksheet and proposed interim GL treatment: post to suspense tax clearing account and schedule payment authorization pending supplier evidence to avoid further late fees. Awaiting sign-off from Accounting Manager.

2026-03-10 09:40 - Accounting Manager (Elena Martinez): Approved interim suspense posting for partial payment up to 60% of USD portion to reduce late penalties, provided vendor signs a payment agreement. Payment team to prepare a one-time JV to move amounts when final allocation confirmed.
source.resolution_notes: Pending: vendor to supply entity split with VAT numbers or separate invoices. Interim action: partial payment authorization approved up to 60% of USD-allocated amount to mitigate late fees; funds to be posted to tax-suspense GL 2109 and reversed when final allocation confirmed. Tax has documented required evidence in Confluence. Next steps: Vendor Ops to obtain signed allocation by 2026-03-16; AP to prepare manual payment once evidence is received.
source.next_steps:
1) Vendor Ops to escalate to GlobalCloud account manager for signed split invoice by 2026-03-16.
2) AP to prepare partial payment template and JV posting once vendor signs agreement.
3) Tax to confirm acceptable evidence format and update rule set to allow supplier-attested allocation for small consolidations.
4) Procurement to add updated PO instruction to future contracts to avoid consolidated ambiguous invoices.
source.notes_internal_reference: Refer to runbook 'Invoice Tax Allocation Runbook' and previous vendor pattern (see INT-151234). Finance owner: Samuel Patel. Escalation path: Accounting Manager -> Head of Finance.

source.summary: Create Slack <-> Confluence bridge: topic-tagging and automatic message translation for cross-region channels
source.description:
Issue summary:
We need a lightweight Slack <-> Confluence bridge that adds structured topic tags to messages in selected cross-region channels and performs on-the-fly translation into the channel participants' preferred languages. This is to improve discoverability of operational discussions and reduce friction for non-EN teams.

Impact:
- Engineers in EU/APAC report slower context handoffs when incidents span regions.
- Important decisions and short runbook fragments posted in Slack are not consistently captured or tagged in Confluence.
- Manual translation or repeated explanation adds latency during incident response.

Goal / Request:
- Implement a bot/service that can: (1) suggest and apply a standardized topic tag (e.g., #deploy, #incident, #billing) to messages based on NLP classification, (2) post a short, tagged excerpt to a configured Confluence space/page (with linkback), and (3) optionally translate the message into a set of configured languages (start with en, fr, de, ja) when the channel has members with those locale preferences.
- Provide an admin UI or slash-commands to opt channels in/out and configure the Confluence mapping.
- The feature should respect DLP: allow opt-out per-channel and avoid sending messages from channels marked "sensitive" to Confluence or third-party translation APIs.
source.background: We previously scoped a general 'bridge' in INT-64812 but that ticket focused on provisioning and broad syncing behavior. This request is narrower: a tagging + translation workflow for cross-region operational channels. SRE and Platform Ops have committed to pilot in three channels: #sre-ops (global), #infra-eu (eu-west), #platform-apac (ap-southeast).
source.steps_to_validate:
1) Deploy bot to dev workspace and invite to test channels.
2) Post a short incident message containing keywords (deploy, rollback, latency).
3) Confirm bot suggests appropriate topic tag and user can approve via button.
4) After approval, confirm the excerpt appears in Confluence page with tag and link.
5) If user has locale set to fr or de, confirm translated copy appears in Slack thread.
6) Validate that channels marked with label 'sensitive' do not forward messages or call translation API.
source.investigation_notes:
- Initial classifier prototype (fastText-based) achieves ~78% precision on a small ops dataset; needs tuning for multi-label cases.
- Translation options evaluated: internal microservice using open-source model vs managed API (latency/cost tradeoff). Managed API has 90% availability SLA but raises privacy concerns unless proxied through our KMS.
- Confluence API rate limits: need batching for high-traffic channels to avoid 429s.
- Auth model: bot will use a service account scoped to specific Confluence spaces; must be added to access control list by Docs team.
comments: Maya Chen (Reporter) - 2026-02-20: Logged initial request after 3 cross-region incidents where non-EN responders missed context. Provided sample messages and suggested tag taxonomy.
Ethan Park (Assignee) - 2026-02-22: Low-cost prototype scheduled. Will run classification on a mirror stream first to validate precision before any writes to Confluence or calling translation APIs.

Nina Patel (Security) - 2026-02-25: Flagging DLP requirement. Any external translation service must be approved by Security; prefer on-premise models behind KMS for pilot. Also require per-channel opt-out and retention controls for Confluence postings.
Docs Team (via @doc-admin) - 2026-02-27: Can provision a sandbox Confluence space and whitelist the service account; need final tag taxonomy. Also request a sample post format for indexing/search.
Ethan Park - 2026-03-05: Prototype classifier results attached in PR; accuracy improved to ~84% after expanding training set. Will present demo on 2026-03-12 sync.
source.acceptance_criteria:
- Bot classifies ops messages into topic tags with >=80% precision on pilot channels.
- Admin can opt channels in/out and map channel->Confluence page via slash-commands or config file.
- Excerpt posts to Confluence include tag metadata and link back to Slack thread.
- Translations appear only for opted-in channels and support at least en/fr/de/ja.
- DLP: channels labeled 'sensitive' must never forward to Confluence or call external translation services.

title: Create Slack <-> Confluence bridge: topic-tagging and automatic message translation for cross-region channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a lightweight Slack <-> Confluence bridge that adds structured topic tags to messages in selected cross-region channels and performs on-the-fly translation into the channel participants' preferred languages. This is to improve discoverability of operational discussions and reduce friction for non-EN teams.

Impact:
- Engineers in EU/APAC report slower context handoffs when incidents span regions.
- Important decisions and short runbook fragments posted in Slack are not consistently captured or tagged in Confluence.
- Manual translation or repeated explanation adds latency during incident response.

Goal / Request:
- Implement a bot/service that can: (1) suggest and apply a standardized topic tag (e.g., #deploy, #incident, #billing) to messages based on NLP classification, (2) post a short, tagged excerpt to a configured Confluence space/page (with linkback), and (3) optionally translate the message into a set of configured languages (start with en, fr, de, ja) when the channel has members with those locale preferences.
- Provide an admin UI or slash-commands to opt channels in/out and configure the Confluence mapping.
- The feature should respect DLP: allow opt-out per-channel and avoid sending messages from channels marked "sensitive" to Confluence or third-party translation APIs.

source.summary: Provision 'Model Safety Review' pod: Confluence spaces, delegated admin groups, and timezone-aware ephemeral Slack standup channels
source.description:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordination with timezone-aware naming and archival automation.

Background and rationale:
- Several new pods will start working on safety evaluations and need consistent docs and low-friction collaboration spaces.
- Current ad-hoc creation leads to inconsistent permissions, missing watchers, and manual cleanup.
- Pods operate across APAC/EMEA/NA timezones; standup channels should be grouped by timezone to reduce noise.

Requested deliverables (high level):
1) Create a Confluence parent space named "Model Safety Review - <pod-name>" for each new pod (template to be applied). The space should have the following: a pre-populated "Evaluation Runbook" page, an "Artifacts & Results" page, and a "Decision Log" page.
2) Provision Confluence groups per pod: safety-<pod-name>-admins (Confluence admin role scoped to the space), safety-<pod-name>-viewers. Add initial users (list below) to appropriate groups.
3) Create Slack channels for each pod using naming pattern: #msr-<pod-name>-standup-<tz> (where <tz> is na / eme / apac). Channels must be created as private channels and have pinned templates for standup prompts.
4) Add automation: a) Add watchers (safety-<pod-name>-viewers) to the Confluence space; b) Configure a scheduled archival policy: Slack channels auto-archive after 90 days of inactivity; Confluence space set to be flagged for review after 180 days with a one-click archive button for platform ops.
5) Wire a simple Slack-to-Confluence bridge for standup summaries: a daily summary message posted to the space's "Daily Standups" page via the existing integration bot.

Initial pod list and membership to provision now:
- pod-name: firebreak-eval -> admins: [Marcus Kim, Lena Ortiz] -> viewers: [Anita Shah, Priya Rao, Carlos Mendes]
- pod-name: redwood-adversarial -> admins: [Jordan Blake] -> viewers: [Marcus Kim, Elena Park]

Acceptance criteria (see separate field for clarity): space templates exist, groups created and scoped, channels created with correct naming/visibility, automations configured and tested, owners notified.
source.acceptance_criteria:
1) Confluence spaces named 'Model Safety Review - <pod-name>' created for both pods and visible to listed viewers.
2) Confluence groups safety-<pod-name>-admins and safety-<pod-name>-viewers exist and have correct members.
3) Slack channels #msr-<pod>-standup-na / -eme / -apac created as private channels for each pod (create only the tz buckets requested; for the two pods: na & eme).
4) Standup prompt template pinned in each channel.
5) Automation: Slack channels set with 90d inactivity auto-archive; Confluence space review workflow attached and tested (triggered review email sent).
6) A daily standup digest from Slack is posted into the Confluence 'Daily Standups' page (validate 2 successful posts).
7) Owners (admins listed in description) confirm access and receive a short onboarding note with links to runbook and channel conventions.
source.implementation_notes:
Suggested steps for Ops team:
- Create Confluence parent space via template: use 'Team Pod Space v2' as base and apply the 'Evaluation Runbook' and 'Decision Log' page blueprints. Set space permissions to: safety-<pod>-admins = Space Admin, safety-<pod>-viewers = View. Add default reader group 'engineering@redwood.com' only if pod owner requests.
- Provision groups in the identity provider (Okta) using naming convention okta:safety-<pod>-admins and sync to Confluence via SCIM. If SCIM not available, create groups in Confluence and attach members manually, then schedule SCIM sync ticket.
- Slack: create private channels and invite the admin list plus the bridge bot account 'msr-bridge-bot'. Pin the standup prompting message template. Set channel topic to include link to space.
- Automation: Use existing housekeeping bot (channel-sweeper) to set auto-archive inactivity threshold to 90 days. For Confluence, attach a scheduled task to create a review flag after 180 days and email space admins.
- Bridge: configure the msr-bridge-bot to post daily summary at 09:00 local pod timezone into Confluence via the REST API key stored in the secrets manager. Test in staging first.
- Security: ensure no external guests are added to private channels. Confirm Confluence space ACLs do not expose confidential artifacts to external groups.

If any of the above requires new infra (SCIM group creation, bot permissions, or Confluence plugin enablement), escalate to Platform Infra with linked issue.
comments: 2026-03-05 - Anita Shah (reporter): Created initial request; please estimate effort and list any blockers.
2026-03-06 - Jordan Blake (assignee): Noted. Will validate SCIM availability for group provisioning and check msr-bridge-bot permissions. Tentative ETA 5 business days.
2026-03-08 - Platform Infra (comment): SCIM group creation will require new mapping. Logging infra ticket INT-562210 and will link once ready.

2026-03-09 - Jordan Blake: Provisioned Confluence spaces in staging and created Slack private channels for 'firebreak-eval' (na) and 'redwood-adversarial' (eme). Need any additional viewer additions to finalize.

source.summary: Org policy deny on aws:TagCondition blocks cross-account ML deploys and GCP service-account impersonation
source.description: Summary: Multiple ML deployment jobs for Dedicated customers failed when the provisioning pipeline attempted to assume a cross-account role and then impersonate a GCP service account. Impact: 6 deployment jobs for two enterprise tenants failed intermittently between 2026-03-01 02:10 UTC and 2026-03-01 03:45 UTC. Cause hypothesis: an AWS Organization Service Control Policy (SCP) with TagCondition-based denies blocks the sts:TagSession or ec2:RunInstances call used by the provisioner, causing the assume-role chain to fail and the downstream GCP impersonation to abort. This prevents creation of ephemeral credentials and blocks the dedicated capacity provisioning flow. User-visible impact: deployment retries surface 403/AccessDenied errors in the console and CI pipelines report "permission denied". Business impact: Dedicated customers in enterprise tier experienced delayed rollouts; no data exposure detected.
steps_to_reproduce: Trigger dedicated-provisioner to create a new ml-fleet node for tenant 'acme-corp' in prod:aws-us-east-1 using the normal onboarding pipeline
Observe the provisioner performing an AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity/AssumeRole call into account A, then attempting to tag the session or launch an instance with session tags
Provisioner then calls the cross-account service-broker which performs GCP service-account impersonation to register the node in GKE cluster in gcp-us-central1
Confirm failure: AWS API returns AccessDenied due to org SCP TagCondition deny; subsequent GCP impersonation fails with 403: 'Service account impersonation denied' in the broker logs
logs: 2026-03-01T02:13:05Z dedicated-provisioner ERROR assume-role failed: AccessDenied: User is not authorized to perform: sts:AssumeRole with provided role ARN due to policy: 'DenyTagConditionOrgPolicy'
2026-03-01T02:13:07Z service-broker ERROR gcp-impersonate failed: 403 Permission denied for serviceAccount: projects/-/serviceAccounts/ml-provisioner@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
comments: 2026-03-01 03:20 - Aisha Khan (SRE): I confirmed the SCP that landed last week contains a Deny with Condition:ForAllValues:StringNotEqualsIfExists on 'aws:RequestTag/owner' that blocks sessions without a matching tag. It looks like the provisioner signs AssumeRole requests without session tags in some codepaths.

2026-03-01 10:05 - Miguel Torres (Infra): Reproduced in a sandbox account by toggling the SCP. When SCP is active, AssumeRole calls that omit TagSession fail. If we include sts:TagSession on the request, it proceeds. Need to decide whether provisioner should always pass tags or whether Org policy should be relaxed for the service principal.

2026-03-02 09:42 - Ben Carter (Assignee): Added temporary feature branch to include owner/env session tags in AssumeRole calls for the provisioner and service-broker. Running internal e2e to validate no other flows break.

2026-03-03 14:10 - Dana Li (Reporter): Customer 'Acme Corp' confirmed reduced failures after we applied a test deployment with the patched tags. They reported 2/2 successful runs during validation window.

2026-03-04 11:30 - Priya Sharma (Security): Noted we should not relax the Org SCP. Preferred approach is to ensure our control-plane services always set required tags and fall back with clear error messages. Also requested a confluence runbook update and audit log entry for any policy-related failures.
investigation_notes: Checked CloudTrail: saw AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity entries returning AccessDenied with 'DenyTagConditionOrgPolicy' for the provisioner principal
Confirmed branch of provisioner omits STS session tags when launching warm instances (optimization codepath)
Validated that adding sts:TagSession permission to the role policy is necessary but insufficient if Org SCP denies by tag; therefore fix must include sending tags + ensuring role allows tagging
Tested in sandbox: patched provisioner to include session tags 'owner' and 'env' and the cross-account assume succeeded and GCP impersonation completed
Checked audit: no anomalous privilege escalations; only deny behavior observed, no credentials leaked
resolution: Workaround (applied 2026-03-02): rolled out patched provisioner binary to prod nodes that include required sts session tags on AssumeRole calls for the affected codepath. This mitigated immediate failures for pending deployments.

Permanent fix (in progress): merge and roll out PR #1982 to ensure all provisioner and service-broker flows include consistent session tags and add unit/integration tests. Target ETA: 2026-03-10 for full rollout.
Security action: Do not change Org SCP. Security will add explicit guidance to Confluence and request an automated alert whenever 'DenyTagConditionOrgPolicy' triggers on core services.
Follow-ups: update runbook steps for 'AssumeRole Tagging' (owner/env), add observability metric for sts:TagSession failures, and schedule a retrospective with Security and Infra owners.
Owner: Ben Carter (Infra) for code rollout; Priya Sharma (Security) for policy/runbook updates

source.summary: Provisional access hold & asset retention for 30-day temp rehire pending probation review
source.description:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a temporary rehire for a former engineer (internal candidate) under a 30-day probation. HR needs a controlled, time-bound access posture: the employee should have only minimal service accounts and read-only console access during the probation window, and their previously issued company laptop must be retained in a secure hold state (not returned to circulation) until the probation completes and audit clearance is confirmed.

Impact:
- Candidate cannot start work until access posture is confirmed (delays go-live by an estimated 3 business days).
- Risk of overprivilege if previous access is reinstated without remediation (previous offboarding was performed but there are known lingering service accounts).

Background:
- Employee: former full-time SWE, rehired as contractor for 30 days pending probation review.
- Offboarding completed 2025-11-12; assets inventoried and laptop returned to inventory record #INV-2025-11-12-319.
- HR request asks to place the laptop on hold (don’t reassign) and to re-enable a minimal set of accounts only after manual approval.

Requested actions (HR):
1) Re-enable corporate email and SSO identity but keep membership in all privileged groups removed.
2) Recreate a scoped service account for CI read-only triggers only (no write permissions).
3) Place laptop INV-2025-11-12-319 into an "Evidence/Hold" tag in asset-management and lock assignment.
4) Do not reissue sudo or elevated IAM roles; block VPN until MFA re-enrollment and manager approval.
5) Schedule an access review at day 14 and day 28 of the probation window.

Constraints and policy questions:
- Does Redwood policy permit holding hardware out of circulation for a probationary rehiring period longer than the standard 14-day quarantine window? HR prefers 30 days to allow audit and manager sign-off.
- If we retain the old laptop on hold but re-enable SSO, do we need to rekey associated KMS/HSM-managed secrets or rotate service-account credentials to avoid potential continuity risks from previous deprovisioning?

Acceptance criteria:
- Identity re-enabled with explicit list of allowed groups and audit events are being emitted to the security log.
- Service account created with explicit deny-write scope and PR created documenting permissions.
- Asset-management entry marked "hold-for-probation" and locked from reassignment.
- Access review reminders scheduled and linked to this ticket.

Next steps requested from assignee:
- Confirm feasibility and provide estimated time to complete each action.
- Call out any policy blockers (KMS rotation, asset-hold duration) and recommended exception process if needed.
investigation_notes: Checked asset history: laptop INV-2025-11-12-319 last assigned to user in 2025-11; device sanitized per offboarding notes. Inventory entry exists and device is in secure storage (Rack B3).
IAM audit: previous user identity was deactivated; several long-lived service tokens were archived. Need to identify tokens tied to user email aliases before re-enabling SSO.
Preliminary security recommendation: perform service-account credential rotation on re-creation; do not reuse archived tokens. Consider ephemeral short-lived creds for the 30-day window.
Identified linked asset tag mismatch: inventory shows asset-tag 'RWI-319' but HR email references 'INV-319'—confirm canonical ID before asset hold action.
steps_taken: Opened a hold request with asset-management and physically placed a retention sticker on Rack B3 bin. (Pending confirmation from warehouse lead.)
Flagged identity in IdP (Okta) as 'pending-reactivation' to prevent auto-provisioning of group memberships until manual approval.
Created placeholder scoped service account 'ci-readonly-INT-824999' with zero privileges; awaiting least-privilege ACL to be applied by infra owner.
comments: 2026-03-01 09:14 - Aisha Malik: HR requests a conservative approach for rehire. Please advise on asset hold policy for 30 days.
2026-03-01 11:02 - Ethan Park: Acknowledged. I'll validate inventory ID and start a scoped service-account template. Need security input on KMS/key rotation.

2026-03-02 08:27 - Lena Ortiz (Security): Recommend rotating any keys associated with the previous identity. If the device was sanitized properly, we can keep it in hold state but re-enabling SSO without rotation is a no-go.
2026-03-03 14:13 - Warehouse Lead (Tom Hsu): Physical hold confirmed on Rack B3; will not issue device unless we get an explicit release note from this ticket.
2026-03-04 10:05 - Ethan Park: Created PR with proposed ACL for ci-readonly-INT-824999: PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/13752
2026-03-04 10:18 - Aisha Malik: HR approves ACL proposal conditionally; wants confirmation that VPN blocked until MFA re-enroll.
source.workaround: If policy prevents 30-day hold, HR requests a 14-day hold with a documented exception: asset retained on-site and candidate starts with fully virtual-only work (no VPN or on-prem access).
source.resolution: In progress — Security approved conditional approach: rotate/issue new service-account credentials and require KMS key rotation only if any archived keys are present. Asset to remain on hold for 30 days pending HR sign-off; warehouse will not reassign. Follow-up: schedule final access review on 2026-03-15 and 2026-03-29.

source.summary: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
source.description: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.
source.steps_to_reproduce: 1) Receive repaired device from depot with NVMe replacement. 2) Power on and complete Windows OOBE. 3) Device auto-enrolls in Intune / Autopilot. 4) Check assetdb entry and compare Disk signature (via 'wmic diskdrive get serialnumber' and 'Get-PnpDevice' combined with disk GUIDs). 5) Observe duplicated Device ID and hostname collision in Autopilot console.
source.logs:
Event log snippets:
- System: Kernel-Boot: Boot device changed: "NVMe0n1" -> partition order: 1,3,2
- MDT/Task Sequence: TSManager: Applied image "CorpWin11-Image" (success)
- Intune MDM: Enrollment token accepted for deviceID: 9f2b-... but assetdb maps to existing asset with device tag 'DLT-7440-234'
- Autopilot: Duplicate hostname collision detected for serial: G7G12345
- Diskpart list: Disk 0 Online, Partition 1=EFI(100MB), Partition 2=MSR, Partition 3=Windows (C:)
(Observed ordering differs between repaired devices and lab reference image).
comments: 2026-03-01 - Priya Shah: Filed after three reports from depot last week; batch ID DPT-2026-03-10. Noticed asset tag matched an existing record but DiskSignature differed.

2026-03-02 - Mateo Alvarez: I pulled two affected devices into bench, captured disk signatures and partition GUIDs. See attached export in Bitbucket: repo/repairs/INT-892345/ (private). Initial hypothesis: NVMe vendor firmware write during reflow resets/reset UUID fields.

2026-03-03 - SRE (Noah Kim): Checked assetdb ingestion pipeline. No recent changes; ingestion keys use TPM UUID + disk signature fallback. In affected samples TPM reports are present but disk signature mismatch triggers fallback path.

2026-03-03 - Imaging (Aisha Patel): Re-imaged an affected unit with the same image, device normalized and registered correctly. Suggests issue occurs only post-repair before depot image re-flash. Potential hardware/firmware ordering artifact.
2026-03-03 - Mateo Alvarez: Requested depot to run vendor NVMe secure-erase workflow rather than reflow; holding further repairs until we confirm.
source.investigation_notes:
Hypotheses:
- NVMe controller/firmware during vendor replacement produces non-deterministic partition/disk GUIDs leading to mismatch with our device fingerprinting which partially uses disk signature as tie-breaker.
- Depot repair process may be cloning an older drive image and then performing a secure erase/partition step that inadvertently reorders partitions.
Commands/observations run:
- get-wmiobject -class Win32_DiskDrive | select SerialNumber,Model
- Get-Partition -DiskNumber 0 | select PartitionNumber,Guid
- compare TPMDeviceID vs DiskSignature across sample set (affected and control)
Findings:
- TPMDeviceID consistent across samples; disk signature different for affected devices.
- Re-imaging normalized disk signature to expected value.
- No kernel driver errors in System event log.
Next steps:
- Request depot to perform NVMe secure-erase + vendor FW update and re-run OOBE before shipping back.
- Add a one-time assetdb mapping override to merge duplicate entries when TPMDeviceID matches but disk signature differs to avoid hostname collision during Autopilot enrollment.
source.resolution: Workaround applied (temporary): Imaging team added a post-repair re-flash step at depot: run vendor secure-erase + FW revision to the drive, then re-image if the device shows disk signature mismatch. This prevents duplicate Device ID creation. Long-term: Engineering to update assetdb ingestion to prioritise TPMDeviceID and add a tolerant merge rule when TPM matches but disk signature mismatches. Depot ops notified and a quick-check script added to depot QC runbook. Follow-up ticket created to modify ingestion logic: INT-892900. Status: mitigation in place; awaiting full ingestion change PR and depot confirmation.

title: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
preview: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.

source.summary: Oncall users missing access to new Grafana dashboards for rollout auto-rollback (SLO integration)
source.description:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration rollout (especially during game day / staging-to-prod expansion). Request: update access groups/permissions so oncall rotations can view the dashboards by default, and document the process so future dashboard additions don’t repeat this.

Dashboards impacted (from observability-pack PR-913):
- "Rollouts: Auto-rollback events"
- "Rollouts: SLO evaluation outcomes"
- "Rollouts: Monitor staleness / missing data"

Who is impacted:
- #eng-oncall and #eng-sre responders
- Service owners paged on rollback events (non-SRE) who are in standard oncall access groups

Impact:
- Increased time-to-diagnosis during auto-rollback events
- Oncall cannot validate whether rollback trigger was legitimate vs monitor stale / suppression in effect

Requested outcome:
1) Add dashboards to the correct Grafana folder with oncall-viewer access.
2) Ensure "Oncall" access groups include permissions for the folder + datasource used by these dashboards.
3) Add short documentation snippet to the oncall readiness checklist / runbook explaining where access is granted and who owns it.
source.steps_to_reproduce:
1) Log into Grafana with a user who is oncall but not in the Observability Admin group (example: standard service-owner oncall).
2) Click the dashboard link posted by incident-bot for an auto-rollback event (or navigate to the "Rollouts" folder).
3) Observe permission error when opening the dashboard.
source.expected_result: Any user in standard oncall access groups (SRE oncall + service-owner oncall) can view rollout auto-rollback dashboards and underlying panels without requesting ad-hoc access during an incident.
source.actual_result: Some oncall users cannot view the dashboards due to missing folder-level permissions (and/or datasource permissions), resulting in a Grafana access denied screen.
source.investigation_notes:
Initial report came from game day follow-ups: several non-SRE service owners were unable to open the dashboard links. SREs with elevated access could view them.

Hypotheses checked:
- Dashboards were created in a restricted folder (e.g., Observability Engineering) rather than the shared "Oncall" or "SRE" folder.
- Datasource permissions: dashboards use the production metrics datasource that is not granted to the service-owner oncall group.

Triage actions:
- Confirmed at least two users in "oncall-service-owners" group cannot access dashboard UID(s) referenced by incident-bot.
- Verified that incident-bot links are correct; this is not a broken URL problem.

Ownership:
- Dashboards shipped via observability-pack; folder placement + permissions owned by Observability/SRE (Aisha Bello).
source.resolution:
Moved the rollout auto-rollback dashboards into the shared "Oncall" Grafana folder and updated folder permissions to include:
- group: sre-oncall (Viewer)
- group: oncall-service-owners (Viewer)

Confirmed datasource permissions for the production metrics datasource include the same groups (Viewer).

Documentation added to the oncall readiness notes in the auto-rollback runbook: where dashboards live, which groups grant access, and the process to request changes (Internal Support -> INT queue) including required approvers (Observability oncall).
comments: 2026-02-24 09:18 PT - Rafael Mendes (reporter): During today’s rollout auto-rollback game day follow-up, two service-owner oncall responders couldn’t open the Grafana dashboards linked by incident-bot (permission denied). We need this fixed before we expand prod enablement beyond the internal route. Links came from #eng-oncall thread 1770311020.

2026-02-24 10:02 PT - Aisha Bello (assignee): Acknowledged. Pulling the dashboard UIDs referenced by incident-bot and checking folder + datasource ACLs. Likely created under Observability Engineering folder by default.

2026-02-24 11:11 PT - Allison Grant: Please make sure we follow the standard folder naming/permissions and avoid adding labels that explode cardinality in dashboard variables. Also, if we move folders, update any runbook links so they don’t 404.

2026-02-25 08:27 PT - Aisha Bello: Confirmed dashboards are under a restricted folder and inherit Observability Admin-only viewer permissions. Plan: move to shared Oncall folder and grant viewer access to sre-oncall + oncall-service-owners. Also verifying the prod metrics datasource is accessible to those groups.

2026-02-26 14:36 PT - Aisha Bello: Changes applied. Tested with a non-admin account in oncall-service-owners: dashboards load and panels render. Incident-bot links still work post-move. Updating runbook section with access guidance.
2026-02-27 09:05 PT - Sean Gallagher: Verified with two oncall responders; access issue resolved. Please keep the documented process short and include who to page if access breaks during an incident.
2026-02-27 09:22 PT - Aisha Bello: Runbook updated with a short "Dashboard access" section (folder + groups + request path). Closing ticket.

source.summary: Prod audit-log extraction request for compliance review of customer data-handling
source.description:
Issue summary: Compliance has requested a scoped extraction of production audit logs and inference metadata to validate BrightLeaf Health's data handling and redaction procedures.

Impact: The extraction must include request/response metadata, inference latency and model-version traces. Raw prompt/response text may contain PHI/PII; we must minimize exposure and follow approved handling controls.

Environment: prod / us-east (hosted Redwood runtime).

Requested window: 2026-02-01 through 2026-02-18 (inclusive).

Requested fields: timestamp, request_id, customer_id (hashed), model_version, token_count, latency_ms, status_code, response_hash. Compliance is requesting a separate mapping file of request_id -> original_customer_id accessible only to Legal. No raw prompt/response text unless explicitly approved after redaction.

Purpose: Third-party compliance review tied to contract clause 4.3. Compliance team (Sophie) needs the extract to demonstrate retention and redaction practices in an audit.

Access model proposed by Compliance: ephemeral extract job run by SRE in a sandboxed account, output encrypted and uploaded to pre-approved SFTP location. Access logged and reviewed.

Approvals required: Security (must confirm extraction script and redaction), Legal (confirm contractual scope), SRE (perform extraction and attest to access controls).

Deliverables requested: encrypted CSV of allowed fields, checksum manifest, extraction runbook, and attestations from SRE/Security/Legal.
comments: 2026-02-20 Sophie Alvarez (Compliance): Opening request. We need to provide proof points for an external audit next week. We're asking for the minimal set of columns listed in description. Legal has greenlit the scope conditionally on redaction. Please advise on feasibility and ETA.

2026-02-20 Ethan Park (SRE): Acknowledged. Initial question: are we allowed to include response_hash mapping to original request text for the internal attest? If not, we'll compute content_hash and avoid raw text. Also need to know whether we should run extraction against hot storage or log-archival buckets.

2026-02-21 Maya Chen (Security): We can't export raw prompts. Security requests a redaction pass that removes/freezes any token-level content and outputs only content_hash and metadata. All extraction must be executed from a dedicated ephemeral worker with no outbound internet except to the approved SFTP. We'll need to rotate keys and capture an access log. Blocking until SRE confirms isolation mechanism.

2026-02-21 Sophie Alvarez (Compliance): Content_hash is fine; Legal will hold the mapping file under escrow. Please flag any fields that would limit the audit. Auditor timeline: they begin review 2026-02-26.

2026-02-22 Ethan Park (SRE): Performed PR prototype for extraction. Proposed approach: run a Lambda-style ephemeral job inside a lockdown VPC, query CloudWatch Logs Insights for request span and join with inference traces stored in S3 (inference-metrics). Script: aws logs start-query + S3 join + redaction step using internal tool redactor v1.2. Uploaded draft PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/3245. Need Security sign-off on IAM role and egress rules.

2026-02-23 Maya Chen (Security): Reviewed PR 3245. Comments left on PR regarding least-privilege IAM. Approved conditional on: 1) no secret keys in logs; 2) redactor uses deterministic salt stored only in HSM; 3) upload destination uses bucket policies to restrict downloads to Legal/Security accounts. Please attach attestation once extraction completes.

2026-02-24 Legal (Daniel O'Neill): Confirmed conditional acceptance. Legal will accept the escrow mapping file under pre-specified NDA. Require that Compliance and Security attest to chain-of-custody for the artifact. If any raw transcript is needed, we will require a separate Approval ticket.

2026-02-24 Ethan Park (SRE): Extraction job scheduled for 2026-02-25 02:00 UTC in a dedicated ephemeral worker. Will upload encrypted output to sftp://audit-uploads.brightleaf.example/ and notify Legal. Creating follow-up runbook ticket INT-5790 to capture steps and retention policy.
source.investigation_notes:
Actions taken:
- Reviewed request and scope with Compliance (2026-02-20).
- Scanned log retention buckets to estimate size: 52GB of candidate records in inference-metrics/us-east for requested window.
- Ran sample CloudWatch Logs Insights query (us-east): 
  > fields @timestamp, requestId, customerId, model, latencyMs, statusCode | filter @timestamp >= 1643673600000 and @timestamp <= 1645036800000 | limit 1000
- Verified that raw prompt text is present in 'request_payload' field in archives older than 90 days; however, hot logs have partial redaction already via pipeline.
- Prototype extraction built in branch platform/extractors/comp-audit. Includes redactor v1.2 call: redact_tool --input s3://inference-metrics/us-east/2026-02 --remove-prompt --output /tmp/redacted.csv
- Performed dry-run on 1000 rows to validate no raw token output. Hashing approach: sha256(salt + requestId). Salt stored in HSM and not exported.
- Dependencies: requires IAM role 'audit-extractor-prod' with s3:GetObject, logs:StartQuery, logs:GetQueryResults, kms:Decrypt (for HSM operations), kms:GenerateDataKey for envelope encryption.
source.resolution:
Pending final execution. Conditions for closure:
- Security signs off on IAM adjustments (done conditionally in PR comments).
- Extraction run completes and output validated (scheduled for 2026-02-25).
- Legal confirms receipt and chain-of-custody (in progress).
Post-resolution actions: create permanent runbook (INT-5790), add automated guardrail to prevent raw-prompt exports, and add an alert to Console for any ad-hoc extraction attempts. Artifact locations: encrypted upload sftp://audit-uploads.brightleaf.example/ and internal backup s3://secure-audit-exports/prod/INT-5789/. Related PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/3245

source.summary: Add RBAC permissions for rollout auto-rollback suppression/override (admin-only)
source.description:
Requester: Felix Schneider (Console views / auditability)

Context
As part of the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration, we are adding:
- Admin-only suppression windows ("do not auto-rollback for X")
- Admin-only manual override actions (pause/rollback) when auto-actions are enabled

We need RBAC updates so these controls are not available to general Console admins or service owners by default, and all usage is auditable.

Current behavior
- In staging, suppression/override endpoints are behind a feature flag but currently rely on a coarse "console_admin" check.
- This is too broad for production; Security requested explicit permissions and audit trail requirements before enabling for production services.

Request
1) Create/define new RBAC permissions (names below are suggested; fine to adjust to match existing RBAC conventions):
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:suppress (create/update/delete suppression window)
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:override (trigger manual pause/rollback action)
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:read_audit (view suppression/override decision details; links to monitor IDs/metrics snapshots)

2) Define default roles / groups:
   - Only members of "Rollouts Admin" (or equivalent) can suppress or override.
   - "SRE Oncall" can override during incident response, but suppression should require Rollouts Admin + justification.
   - Service owners should NOT get suppress/override by default.

3) Approval + audit requirements
   - Any suppression/override must require a free-text justification (min 20 chars) and should be recorded as an audit event with:
     - actor (user + auth principal)
     - permission used
     - target scope (rollout_id and/or service/route/model/region)
     - reason/justification
     - timestamp + request_id/correlation_id
   - Audit events must be retained per existing auto-action audit trail schema.

Acceptance criteria
- Console UI buttons for suppression/override are hidden/disabled unless permission is present.
- API returns 403 with clear error message when permission is missing.
- Audit logging is emitted for successful actions (and ideally for denied attempts with actor + target).
- Security sign-off from Kevin Osei before prod enablement.

Notes
This is a gating item for enabling suppression/override in production as part of the staged enablement plan. Please coordinate changes with the Console backend + policy/control plane owners so permission names are consistent across layers.
comments: 2026-02-08 (Felix Schneider): Filing per Security feedback from last review. We need explicit RBAC permissions before we flip the prod flag for admin override/suppression. Suggested permission names included in ticket; open to better naming if RBAC has a standard pattern.

2026-02-09 (Ethan Park): Acknowledged. I’ll coordinate with Benji/Jordan on policy-engine enforcement points and with Hannah on Console UI gating. We already have an authz hook in the control plane; should be straightforward to add two permissions + a read permission for audit details.

2026-02-10 (Kevin Osei): Security requirement: justification must be required and stored with the audit event. Also need a clear distinction between 'override' vs 'suppress' because suppression can hide regressions. Prefer: override allowed for SRE Oncall; suppress only allowed for Rollouts Admin group. Please ensure denied attempts are logged (at least to security audit stream) to detect misuse.

2026-02-11 (Sean Gallagher): From SRE perspective: override is needed for oncall mitigation (esp. when auto-rollback is suppressed due to known incident or monitor staleness). Agree with Kevin: suppression should be tighter than override, and must have a short TTL by default (e.g., max 2h) unless Rollouts Admin extends with explicit justification.

2026-02-13 (Ethan Park): Draft implementation plan: (1) add new permissions in RBAC service; (2) wire enforcement in Console API endpoints for suppression/override; (3) ensure control plane also checks permission server-side (defense in depth); (4) emit audit events per ADR-0128. Targeting end of week for merge + staging verification.

2026-02-17 (Felix Schneider): Quick UX note: UI will show disabled action with tooltip 'Requires Rollouts Admin permission' and link to internal access request. Also adding field validation for justification length + required.

2026-02-18 (Ethan Park): Implemented permission checks in control plane + console API. Added new RBAC group mapping: Rollouts Admin -> suppress + override + read_audit; SRE Oncall -> override + read_audit. Waiting on Security sign-off after staging verification. Related PRs: pr-28511, pr-28435.

2026-02-20 (Kevin Osei): Reviewed staging events: audit payload includes actor, permission, target, justification, request_id. Looks good. Please ensure max suppression TTL enforced at policy layer (not just UI). Approving from Security side contingent on TTL guardrail being present.

2026-02-21 (Ethan Park): Added server-side guardrail: suppression TTL max 2h unless actor has suppress_extended permission (granted only to Rollouts Admin; not to SRE Oncall). Staging re-verified. Marking resolved; will follow release train enablement checklist with Connor.
source.resolution: RBAC permissions added for rollout auto-rollback suppression/override. Implemented enforcement in Console API + control plane, updated role/group mappings (Rollouts Admin vs SRE Oncall), and ensured justification is required and recorded in audit events. Security approved after staging verification; ready for production enablement behind feature flag per release plan.

source.summary: Audit log pipeline alerts too noisy after rollout (drop-rate/latency) — tune thresholds + update runbook guidance
source.description:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call load and alert fatigue for #eng-oncall / #eng-sre.
- Several pages triggered during expected/benign conditions (brief exporter backpressure, transient ingestion backlog recoveries, low-volume tenants).
- Risk: real incidents may get ignored or delayed due to repeated false positives.

What we’re seeing
- Drop-rate alert firing during short ingestion backlogs or during exporter retries, even when events are later delivered (at-least-once semantics).
- Latency alert firing on p95 spikes during low traffic periods, especially in regions with fewer events.
- Alerts firing per-dimension (region/service) without sufficient volume gating.

Scope
- Tune thresholds and evaluation windows for audit log pipeline alerts.
- Add volume gating / minimum sample requirements.
- Update the audit pipeline incident runbook to reflect new alert semantics and include a ‘noise triage’ checklist.

Notes / Context
- The audit log delivery SLO and dashboards were recently rolled out; the current alerting configuration appears overly sensitive.
- This ticket is intended to adjust alerting behavior without changing core pipeline delivery semantics.

Proposed changes (implementation guidance)
1) Drop-rate alert
- Change evaluation from short-window percentage to burn-rate style alerting with multiple windows (fast + slow) to reduce flapping.
- Add minimum-event-volume gating (do not page if below N events in window).
- Keep a lower-severity warning notification for early signals (non-paging).

2) Latency alert
- Page only on sustained p95 delivery latency above threshold for >= 15m (instead of 5m), plus volume gating.
- Prefer region-aggregated SLO for paging; keep per-region/per-service as non-paging signals.

3) Exporter queue/backpressure
- Convert “queue depth” alerts to warnings unless backlog grows monotonically for >= 20m.
- Ensure dashboards show “ingestion backlog vs exporter backlog” to route the incident correctly.

Acceptance Criteria
- Paging alerts align with actionable incidents (sustained drop-rate or sustained high delivery latency).
- On-call pages reduce materially (target: <2 pages/week for audit pipeline absent real incident).
- Runbook updated with new thresholds, triage steps, and links to the relevant dashboards.

Owner / Reviewers
- Owner: Sean Gallagher (SRE)
- Consult: Rafael Mendes (runbook), Aisha Bello (alerting/dashboards), Logan Wright (coverage telemetry impacts)
comments: 2026-02-12 (Aisha Bello): Flagging this as an internal quality-of-life issue for on-call. We’ve had 6 pages in 48 hours for audit pipeline ‘drop rate’ but no customer-visible impact (events delivered within ~10–25m). Requesting SRE to tune alert windows + add volume gating.

2026-02-13 (Sean Gallagher): Agreed—current config is too twitchy. The drop-rate alert is effectively measuring short-term backlog, not true loss. I’ll propose burn-rate alerts + a minimum sample threshold, and will split paging vs warning signals.

2026-02-14 (Rafael Mendes): Please update the runbook with explicit guidance on ‘backlog != loss’ and a step to check exporter retry metrics before escalating. I can review wording once thresholds are finalized.

2026-02-17 (Sean Gallagher): Implemented changes in alert definitions: (1) drop-rate paging now requires sustained burn-rate across fast+slow windows; (2) latency paging requires >=15m sustained p95 above threshold with min volume; (3) per-region alerts demoted to warning. Requesting Aisha to sanity-check in dashboards.

2026-02-18 (Aisha Bello): Reviewed in dashboards—looks much better. Alert now stays quiet during brief retry storms. We still get a warning in Slack when exporter backlog spikes, which is fine. +1 to ship.
2026-02-19 (Sean Gallagher): Runbook updated with triage checklist + revised thresholds. Closing as resolved; we’ll monitor page volume for a week and reopen if needed.
source.resolution: Adjusted audit log pipeline paging alerts to reduce flapping and align with actionable conditions: added volume gating, switched drop-rate paging to burn-rate multi-window evaluation, increased latency alert evaluation window, and demoted per-dimension signals to warnings. Updated the audit pipeline incident runbook with new thresholds and a noise-triage checklist.

source.summary: Request access for Security + GRC to Audit Log Coverage Dashboard (least-privilege roles)
source.description:
Requesting access for Security and GRC to the new “Audit Log Coverage (Sensitive Actions)” dashboard used for evidence collection and ongoing monitoring.

Business need
- SOC 2 / ISO-aligned evidence: demonstrate monitoring + coverage tracking for sensitive actions audit events.
- Security incident response: ability to quickly validate whether expected audit events are being emitted for specific event_types/services.

Requested access (least privilege)
1) Read-only access to dashboard(s) + saved views.
2) No ability to edit dashboards, create alerts, or query raw log payloads.
3) Access should allow viewing:
   - expected event types vs observed
   - per-service coverage breakdown
   - time window filters (last 24h / 7d / 30d)
   - links out to runbook for regressions
4) Access should NOT allow:
   - exporting raw event bodies containing target identifiers or other potentially sensitive context
   - writing/modifying Grafana folders/dashboards

Users / groups
- Olga Petrov (GRC)
- Daniel Carter (Compliance evidence support)
- Naomi Feldman (Product - Security & Compliance)
- Marcus Lin (Security Eng exec owner) (optional)

Acceptance criteria
- A documented role/group exists for Security+GRC coverage dashboard viewing.
- Access is granted to the above users (or an org group) within 2 business days.
- Instructions exist for future access requests (who approves, what scope).

Notes
- This is internal-only access; not customer-facing.
- The dashboard is referenced by the coverage regression runbook and will be linked in future evidence collection checklists.
source.comments:
2026-02-07 09:18 - Olga Petrov (Reporter)
Opened per SOC2 evidence planning. We need read-only dashboard access before the next control walkthrough. Please keep scope minimal (view-only) and avoid raw payload exposure.

2026-02-07 10:02 - Logan Wright (Assignee)
Acknowledged. Can you confirm if GRC needs access to underlying data source explore/query, or only the published dashboard panels?

2026-02-07 10:26 - Olga Petrov
Dashboard panels only is sufficient. Explore/query is not required and is actually a risk for evidence reviewers (too much surface area).

2026-02-07 11:11 - Nadia Rahman (Telemetry)
FYI the coverage dashboard is backed by metrics in the telemetry warehouse and a Grafana folder (“Audit Logging / Coverage”). There’s no raw event bodies in those panels, but some panels include dimensions like service_name, event_type, and tenant_tier.

2026-02-08 14:40 - Allison Grant (Observability Standards)
+1 on limiting Explore. Recommend a dedicated Grafana role + folder permission: View on folder, no edit, no admin. Also ensure they can’t create alerts from panels (alert spam risk).

2026-02-09 09:05 - Logan Wright
Proposed approach:
- Create Grafana team: sec-grc-audit-coverage
- Folder permission on “Audit Logging / Coverage”: Viewer
- Datasource permissions: allow only “Query” via dashboard, not Explore (Grafana RBAC setting)
- Disable alert creation for this team
Will implement today pending Security Eng approval.

2026-02-09 09:22 - Marcus Lin (Security Eng)
Approved with conditions:
1) No access to raw audit logs index.
2) No Explore access.
3) Ensure dashboard does not include customer identifiers beyond tenant_tier. If any panels show tenant_id/project_id, please remove or hash.

2026-02-10 16:13 - Logan Wright
Implemented access controls:
- Created Grafana team: sec-grc-audit-coverage
- Added users: Olga Petrov, Daniel Carter, Naomi Feldman
- Folder “Audit Logging / Coverage”: Viewer access granted to team
- Explore access disabled for this team (dashboard-only)
- Alert creation disabled for team
Also reviewed panels: no tenant_id/project_id displayed. Dimensions limited to service_name, event_type, deployment_mode, region, and tenant_tier.

2026-02-11 08:47 - Daniel Carter
Confirmed access works. We can view the dashboard and adjust time ranges, but cannot edit panels or create alerts. This meets evidence needs.

2026-02-12 10:03 - Olga Petrov
Confirmed. Please add a short note to the runbook on how to request access going forward (approver + scope). After that, okay to close.

2026-02-12 14:20 - Logan Wright
Added runbook note (Audit Log Coverage Regression Runbook) under “Access / Permissions” section:
- Request via internal-support ticket
- Approver: Observability Standards owner (Allison Grant) + Security Eng (Marcus Lin) for scope changes
- Default grant: viewer-only, dashboard-only
Closing ticket.
source.resolution: Created dedicated Grafana viewer-only team (sec-grc-audit-coverage) and granted folder-level Viewer permissions to “Audit Logging / Coverage” dashboards. Disabled Explore and alert creation for the team to maintain least privilege. Added documentation in the coverage regression runbook for future access requests and approvals.
source.next_steps: If additional evidence viewers are added, request should reference this ticket and default to the same viewer-only scope. Any request for Explore or additional datasources requires explicit Security Eng approval.
source.security_review: Approved by Marcus Lin on 2026-02-09 with least-privilege constraints (no raw logs, no Explore, no customer identifiers).

source.summary: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
source.description:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
   - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
   - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
   - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
   - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
   - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
   - No ability to edit gating thresholds, disable gating globally, or directly apply configs outside the approved override workflow
3) Scope:
   - Hosted API (prod) routes/accounts participating in limited rollout
   - Console UI + underlying approval endpoint

Requested users to be added (initial set):
- Paula Mendes (PM, Rollouts/telemetry) — backup approver for business-hours incidents
- Sean Gallagher (SRE lead) — oncall escalation approver for after-hours/high-severity customer impact

Constraints / policy alignment:
- Overrides must be time-bound and require reason + ticket link per policy
- Approver actions must be captured in audit logs (who/when/what/expiration)
- Prefer SSO group mapping if available; otherwise Console-native group assignment is acceptable for beta

Why now:
We had a near-miss where an optimization change needed rapid override to unblock a customer, but the only available path was a full admin role (too broad). We want an audited, limited approver role before expanding rollout.

Acceptance criteria:
- Named users can approve/deny override requests in Console
- Actions write audit events visible in audit log export
- Users cannot perform broader admin actions (validated via negative test)
- Security sign-off on scope/permissions

Notes:
If feasible, also include ability for Release Engineering oncall to temporarily be added during incident response with a 24h expiration on membership.
comments: 2026-02-04 09:18 PT — Aditya Rao (Reporter): Creating INT ticket per leadership request. We need real approvers (not console-admin) for time-bound overrides before we widen limited prod rollout. Requested initial approvers: Paula Mendes + Sean Gallagher.

2026-02-04 10:02 PT — Marcus Lin (Assignee): Acknowledged. Please confirm whether these users already have any elevated Console roles. Also confirm if the override approval endpoint is already enforcing 'override_approver' vs 'admin' (PR-19537 mentions admin-only guardrails).

2026-02-04 10:21 PT — Aditya Rao: Both currently have standard Console access only (viewer/editor depending on team). PR-19537 in our stack currently checks for 'console_admin' OR 'quality_gate_override_approver' flag (feature-flagged). We need the role/group created and wired to SSO (preferred) or assigned manually for beta.

2026-02-04 11:05 PT — Marcus Lin: Proposed approach: create a dedicated Console RBAC role with only the override approval permissions + audit read for override events. No access to policy/threshold admin. Will coordinate with Platform (Jordan Lee / Benji Okafor) to ensure permission boundary is correct.

2026-02-05 13:47 PT — Marcus Lin: Platform confirmed permission set exists behind feature flag: PERM_OPTIMIZE_OVERRIDE_APPROVE + PERM_OPTIMIZE_GATE_READ + PERM_AUDITLOG_READ(override.*). Proceeding with SSO group mapping request to IT (internal). For beta, we can also assign directly in Console until SSO sync is in place.

2026-02-06 09:32 PT — Sean Gallagher: Adding note from SRE: please ensure the role can view gate failure reasons/artifacts, otherwise oncall can't make an informed approval decision. Also please ensure approvals are searchable in audit logs by route/account.

2026-02-07 16:10 PT — Marcus Lin: Completed: created group optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers and mapped to Console role quality_gate_override_approver. Added Paula Mendes + Sean Gallagher. Verified they can approve/deny override requests and cannot access admin policy pages. Audit events present (override.requested / override.approved / override.denied) with actor + expiration.
2026-02-10 10:06 PT — Aditya Rao: Confirmed in Console: both users can approve overrides; cannot change gating policy; approval emits audit log event with expiration + linked gate_run_id. Closing.
source.resolution: Created and assigned a least-privilege Console RBAC role/group for Quality Gate override approvals; added initial approvers (Paula Mendes, Sean Gallagher); verified audit logging and negative permissions.
source.security_review: Approved (least privilege; audited actions; no global gating disable).
